parsing_res_list.block_content: Influence of triplet excitons on the  lifetime of polymer-based organic  light emitting diodes

In this work,the harmful effect of tripletexcitons on the lifetime of poly(p-phenylene vinylene) (PPV)-based organic light emitting diodes is demonstrated. A model system has been designed in which the triplet population in a PPV derivative is increased by blending thepolymer with thetriplet sensitizer

1Introduction Despite the progress made in thefield ofpolymer-basedorganiclightemitting diodes(PLEDs)during thelast years,continuing efforts in research are necessary forasubstantialbreakthrough incommercialization.Thelifetimeandstabilityofthe devicesarestilloneof themainissues inneedofimprovement[1-5].Usually,luminance degradationandanincrease inoperatingvoltage with prolonged electrical drivingaretypicalfeaturesof electricalfatigue in polymer-basedOLEDs [4].Therebythe device lifetime is normallydefined as the $t_{50}$ orhalf lifetimethe time in which the luminance decreases to 50% from the initialvalueduring operationatconstantcurrent.

According to spinstatistics,theratioof singletto triplet excitonsinOLEDs is1:3.However,differingratioshave been reportedfor many materials [6-9]but no consensuson thevaluehasbeenreached.Nevertheless,duetothehigh lifetimeoftripletexcitonsontheorderof several usto ms[10,11],hightripletdensitiesarereachedinfluorescent devices. Theinfluence ofthese non-radiatively decaying excitons onthe lifetime [12]orefficiency [13-15]of fluorescent OLEDs has been investigated in onlyfew publications.Kingetal.[12],e.g.,reportedanincreaseinthe $t_{50}$ lifetimeaswellasa decreaseintheinitialluminescence

platinum (I) octaethylporphyrine ketone (PtOEPK). The increase in the triplet concentration of thepolymer affects both the initial decay and the long-term evolution of luminance and leads to a drastic acceleration of device degradation,reflected in a dramatically shortened lifetime.

Inthiscontribution,theeffectoftripletexcitonsonthe degradationofOLEDsisstudiedinaPLEDinwhichthe polymer isblendedwitha triplet sensitizer.Thepresenceof the sensitizer leads to the enhancement of the triplet concentration in the matrix semiconductor. The impact of the higher tripletconcentrationondevice lifetime is monitored.Asaresult,anaccelerateddegradation duetotheincreased triplet-to-singletratiowillbereported.

2 Experimental Thisstudyfocuses on devicesbased onthepoly(p-phenylenevinylene)(PpV)derivative $\mathrm{OC}_{3}\mathrm{C}_{8}$ PPV(poly(2-propoxy-5-(2-ethylhexyloxy)-phenylenevinylene),whichisusedbothin pristinestateandblendedwiththe triplet sensitizer materialplatinum(I) octaethylporphyrine ketone(PtOEPK) purchasedfromLivchem Logistics GmbH.ThePLEDsconsistofanITOanode,aPEDOT:PSShole injection layer (40 nm), an $OC_{3}C_{8}-PPV$ layer(130nm)anda Cacathode(20nm)protectedbyanAllayer(100nm).
